parameters.command:
=# =================================================
# INPUTS (n8n variables / parâmetros simples)
# =================================================

# --- PDF ---
PDF_URL="{{ $json.PDF_URL }}"

# --- Render ---
DPI={{ $json.DPI }}              # 150 | 300 | 400
IMAGE_FORMAT="{{ $json.IMAGE_FORMAT }}"      # png | jpg
QUALITY={{ $json.QUALITY }}             # usado apenas para jpg

# --- Pages ---
PAGE_MODE="{{ $json.PAGE_MODE }}"         # all | single | range
PAGE_FROM="{{ $json.PAGE_FROM }}"            # ex: 1
PAGE_TO="{{ $json.PAGE_TO }}"              # ex: 2

# --- Output ---
OUTPUT_TYPE="{{ $json.OUTPUT_TYPE }}"     # local | base64
OUT_DIR="{{ $json.OUT_DIR }}"
BASE64_PREFIX={{ $json.BASE64_PREFIX }}     # true | false
FILE_PREFIX="{{ $json.FILE_PREFIX }}"

# --- Limits ---
MAX_PAGES={{ $json.MAX_PAGES }}

# --- Merge ---
MERGE_PAGES={{ $json.MERGE_PAGES }}     # true | false

# =================================================
# Paths
# =================================================
PDF="{{ $json.PDF_DOWNLOAD_PATH }}"
PY="/tmp/pdf_to_images.py"

mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR"

# =================================================
# Download PDF (mantido como no script funcional)
# =================================================
wget -q -O "$PDF" "$PDF_URL" || exit 1

# =================================================
# Python script (embedded)
# =================================================
cat > "$PY" << 'EOF'
import subprocess
import sys
import os
import json
import base64

PDF_PATH      = sys.argv[1]
OUT_DIR       = sys.argv[2]
DPI           = int(sys.argv[3])
FORMAT        = sys.argv[4]
QUALITY       = int(sys.argv[5])
PAGE_MODE     = sys.argv[6]
PAGE_FROM     = sys.argv[7] or None
PAGE_TO       = sys.argv[8] or None
OUTPUT_TYPE   = sys.argv[9]
BASE64_PREFIX = sys.argv[10].lower() == "true"
MAX_PAGES     = int(sys.argv[11])
FILE_PREFIX   = sys.argv[12]
MERGE_PAGES   = sys.argv[13].lower() == "true"

PREFIX = os.path.join(OUT_DIR, f"{FILE_PREFIX}-page")

# -------------------------------
# Build pdftoppm command
# -------------------------------
cmd = ["pdftoppm", "-r", str(DPI)]

if FORMAT == "png":
    cmd.append("-png")
elif FORMAT in ("jpg", "jpeg"):
    cmd.append("-jpeg")
    cmd += ["-jpegopt", f"quality={QUALITY}"]
else:
    raise Exception("Invalid image format")

if PAGE_MODE == "single" and PAGE_FROM:
    cmd += ["-f", PAGE_FROM, "-l", PAGE_FROM]
elif PAGE_MODE == "range" and PAGE_FROM and PAGE_TO:
    cmd += ["-f", PAGE_FROM, "-l", PAGE_TO]
# PAGE_MODE == all → nada

cmd += [PDF_PATH, PREFIX]

subprocess.check_call(cmd)

# -------------------------------
# Collect files
# -------------------------------
ext = "png" if FORMAT == "png" else "jpg"

files = sorted(
    f for f in os.listdir(OUT_DIR)
    if f.startswith(f"{FILE_PREFIX}-page-") and f.endswith("." + ext)
)

if len(files) == 0:
    raise Exception("No pages generated")

if len(files) > MAX_PAGES:
    raise Exception("Max pages limit exceeded")

result_files = []

# -------------------------------
# Merge pages into single image (optional)
# -------------------------------
if MERGE_PAGES:
    merged_output = os.path.join(OUT_DIR, f"{FILE_PREFIX}-merged.{ext}")

    # Use ImageMagick if available
    merge_cmd = ["convert"]

    for f in files:
        merge_cmd.append(os.path.join(OUT_DIR, f))

    merge_cmd += ["-append", merged_output]

    subprocess.check_call(merge_cmd)

    entry = {"path": merged_output}

    if OUTPUT_TYPE == "base64":
        with open(merged_output, "rb") as img:
            b64 = base64.b64encode(img.read()).decode()
            if BASE64_PREFIX:
                mime = "image/png" if FORMAT == "png" else "image/jpeg"
                b64 = f"data:{mime};base64," + b64
            entry["base64"] = b64

    result_files = [entry]

else:
    for f in files:
        fpath = os.path.join(OUT_DIR, f)
        entry = {"path": fpath}

        if OUTPUT_TYPE == "base64":
            with open(fpath, "rb") as img:
                b64 = base64.b64encode(img.read()).decode()
                if BASE64_PREFIX:
                    mime = "image/png" if FORMAT == "png" else "image/jpeg"
                    b64 = f"data:{mime};base64," + b64
                entry["base64"] = b64

        result_files.append(entry)

# -------------------------------
# Output JSON
# -------------------------------
result = {
    "status": "success",
    "url": PDF_PATH,
    "dpi": DPI,
    "format": FORMAT,
    "page_mode": PAGE_MODE,
    "merge_pages": MERGE_PAGES,
    "pages": len(result_files),
    "files": result_files
}

print(json.dumps(result))
EOF

# =================================================
# Execute
# =================================================
python3 "$PY" \
  "$PDF" \
  "$OUT_DIR" \
  "$DPI" \
  "$IMAGE_FORMAT" \
  "$QUALITY" \
  "$PAGE_MODE" \
  "$PAGE_FROM" \
  "$PAGE_TO" \
  "$OUTPUT_TYPE" \
  "$BASE64_PREFIX" \
  "$MAX_PAGES" \
  "$FILE_PREFIX" \
  "$MERGE_PAGES" \
  2>/dev/null

nodes.name: Sobe as imagens no S3 da AWS

parameters.content:
## 01 - INTERFACE DE ENTRADA & PARAMS Comentário:
-> Ponto de entrada da API. Pode ser acionado via Webhook (JWT Auth).
-> O nó Normaliza dados (Code) é o cérebro da preparação: ele gera o FILE_PREFIX único via timestamp para evitar colisão de arquivos no servidor.
-> Define os caminhos dinâmicos de diretório (OUT_DIR) e o local temporário do PDF (PDF_DOWNLOAD_PATH)."

## 02 - MOTOR DE RENDERIZAÇÃO (LINUX/PYTHON)
-> Execução de baixo nível no servidor (Self-Hosted).
-> Download: O comando wget busca o PDF da URL fornecida e salva no caminho temporário.
-> Conversão: Um script Python embarcado orquestra o utilitário pdftoppm.
-> Parâmetros: Suporta controle de DPI (qualidade), formato (PNG/JPG) e seleção de páginas (all/single/range)."

## 03 - GESTÃO DE BINÁRIOS & ARMAZENAMENTO 
Transforma o processamento local em recursos acessíveis:
-> Read/Write Files: Lê as imagens geradas pelo script Python do sistema de arquivos (/n8n_data) e as carrega como objetos binários no n8n.
-> AWS S3: Faz o upload automático das imagens convertidas para o bucket da MIA (CDN), gerando os links públicos para uso.

nodes.name: Verifica se faz o merge das pgs
Verifica se faz o merge das pgs1

main.node: Sobe as imagens no S3 da AWS
Verifica se faz o merge das pgs1

=# =================================================
# INPUTS (n8n variables / parâmetros simples)
# =================================================

# --- PDF ---
PDF_URL="{{ $json.PDF_URL }}"

# --- Render ---
DPI={{ $json.DPI }}              # 150 | 300 | 400

OUTPUT_TYPE="{{ $json.OUTPUT_TYPE }}"     # local | base64
OUT_DIR="{{ $json.OUT_DIR }}"
BASE64_PREFIX={{ $json.BASE64_PREFIX }}     # true | false
FILE_PREFIX="{{ $json.FILE_PREFIX }}"

# --- Limits ---
MAX_PAGES={{ $json.MAX_PAGES }}

# --- Merge ---
MERGE_PAGES={{ $json.MERGE_PAGES }}     # true | false

# =================================================
# Paths
# =================================================
PDF="{{ $json.PDF_DOWNLOAD_PATH }}"
PY="/tmp/pdf_to_images.py"

mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR"

# =================================================
# Download PDF (mantido como no script funcional)
# =================================================
wget -q -O "$PDF" "$PDF_URL" || exit 1

# =================================================
# Python script (embedded)
# =================================================
cat > "$PY" << 'EOF'
import subprocess
import sys
import os
import json
import base64

PDF_PATH      = sys.argv[1]
OUT_DIR       = sys.argv[2]

FORMAT        = sys.argv[4]
QUALITY       = int(sys.argv[5])
PAGE_MODE     = sys.argv[6]
PAGE_FROM     = sys.argv[7] or None
PAGE_TO       = sys.argv[8] or None
OUTPUT_TYPE   = sys.argv[9]
BASE64_PREFIX = sys.argv[10].lower() == "true"
MAX_PAGES     = int(sys.argv[11])
FILE_PREFIX   = sys.argv[12]
MERGE_PAGES   = sys.argv[13].lower() == "true"

# Build pdftoppm command
# -------------------------------
cmd = ["pdftoppm", "-r", str(DPI)]

if FORMAT == "png":
    cmd.append("-png")
elif FORMAT in ("jpg", "jpeg"):
    cmd.append("-jpeg")

else:
    raise Exception("Invalid image format")

if PAGE_MODE == "single" and PAGE_FROM:
    cmd += ["-f", PAGE_FROM, "-l", PAGE_FROM]
elif PAGE_MODE == "range" and PAGE_FROM and PAGE_TO:
    cmd += ["-f", PAGE_FROM, "-l", PAGE_TO]
# PAGE_MODE == all → nada

cmd += [PDF_PATH, PREFIX]

subprocess.check_call(cmd)

# -------------------------------
# Collect files
# -------------------------------
ext = "png" if FORMAT == "png" else "jpg"

files = sorted(
    f for f in os.listdir(OUT_DIR)
    if f.startswith(f"{FILE_PREFIX}-page-") and f.endswith("." + ext)
)

if len(files) == 0:
    raise Exception("No pages generated")

if len(files) > MAX_PAGES:
    raise Exception("Max pages limit exceeded")

result_files = []

# -------------------------------
# Merge pages into single image (optional)
# -------------------------------
if MERGE_PAGES:
    merged_output = os.path.join(OUT_DIR, f"{FILE_PREFIX}-merged.{ext}")

    # Use ImageMagick if available
    merge_cmd = ["convert"]

    for f in files:
        merge_cmd.append(os.path.join(OUT_DIR, f))

    merge_cmd += ["-append", merged_output]

    subprocess.check_call(merge_cmd)

    entry = {"path": merged_output}

    if OUTPUT_TYPE == "base64":
        with open(merged_output, "rb") as img:
            b64 = base64.b64encode(img.read()).decode()
            if BASE64_PREFIX:
                mime = "image/png" if FORMAT == "png" else "image/jpeg"
                b64 = f"data:{mime};base64," + b64
            entry["base64"] = b64

    result_files = [entry]

else:
    for f in files:
        fpath = os.path.join(OUT_DIR, f)
        entry = {"path": fpath}

        if OUTPUT_TYPE == "base64":
            with open(fpath, "rb") as img:
                b64 = base64.b64encode(img.read()).decode()
                if BASE64_PREFIX:
                    mime = "image/png" if FORMAT == "png" else "image/jpeg"
                    b64 = f"data:{mime};base64," + b64
                entry["base64"] = b64

        result_files.append(entry)

# -------------------------------
# Output JSON
# -------------------------------
result = {
    "status": "success",
    "url": PDF_PATH,
    "dpi": DPI,
    "format": FORMAT,
    "page_mode": PAGE_MODE,
    "merge_pages": MERGE_PAGES,
    "pages": len(result_files),
    "files": result_files
}

print(json.dumps(result))
EOF

# =================================================
# Execute
# =================================================
python3 "$PY" \
  "$PDF" \
  "$OUT_DIR" \
  "$DPI" \
  "$IMAGE_FORMAT" \
  "$QUALITY" \
  "$PAGE_MODE" \
  "$PAGE_FROM" \
  "$PAGE_TO" \
  "$OUTPUT_TYPE" \
  "$BASE64_PREFIX" \
  "$MAX_PAGES" \
  "$FILE_PREFIX" \
  "$MERGE_PAGES" \
  2>/dev/null
